text.title: API Versioning Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the api-versioning methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Additive changes (new field, new optional input, new endpoint) NEVER trigger a new major version. Bump only on breaking semantic changes.
rule.rationale: About 90% of API changes are additive; version ceremony for them wastes cycles and confuses consumers.

rule.statement: Default to URL path versioning (/api/vN/). Never use query-param versioning for cacheable endpoints.
rule.rationale: URL path is cache-friendly (CDN), debuggable (browser address bar), and is what Stripe/Twilio/GitHub all use.

rule.statement: From the day a route is deprecated, emit Deprecation, Sunset, and Link: rel=successor-version headers on every response.
rule.rationale: Headers are machine-readable; SDK maintainers and observability platforms detect them automatically.

rule.statement: Track per-version traffic via a Prometheus label (http_requests_total{version="vN"}). Do not sunset until the version's traffic is zero or negligible for 30 consecutive days.
rule.rationale: You cannot sunset what you cannot measure; removing a version with live clients causes immediate incidents regardless of announced dates.

rule.statement: Sunset in two PRs: (1) the route returns 410 Gone with a body pointing at the successor; (2) only after 30 days of zero traffic, delete the handler code.
rule.rationale: Two-PR sunset prevents accidental restoration and gives clients a final machine-readable signal before code disappears.

rule.statement: v1 routes live in a read-only module after publication. v2 is a separate module; DRY-ing v1 and v2 handlers is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Shared code silently introduces v1 breaking changes when v2 evolves, defeating the entire versioning contract.

rule.statement: If the pending change is additive OR the API is internal-only with a single redeployable consumer OR the stack is GraphQL/protobuf, skip this methodology entirely.
rule.rationale: Forcing version ceremony where it does not pay back creates noise and wastes review time.
